instruction: Implement a parallel version using ParlayLib to compute the maximum element in a sequence.
output: auto max_val = parlay::reduce(seq, parlay::maxm<int>());

instruction: Implement a parallel version using ParlayLib to compute the sum of squares of a sequence.
output: auto sum_sq = parlay::reduce(parlay::map(seq, [] (int x) { return x * x; }));

instruction: Generate a sequence of ones using ParlayLib.
output: auto ones = parlay::tabulate(n, [] (size_t i) { return 1; });

instruction: Apply ParlayLib to reverse a sequence.
output: auto reversed = parlay::reverse(seq);

instruction: Apply the absolute value to each element in a sequence using ParlayLib.
output: auto abs_vals = parlay::map(seq, [] (int x) { return std::abs(x); });

instruction: Implement a parallel version using ParlayLib to count how many elements are greater than 10.
output: auto count = parlay::count_if(seq, [] (int x) { return x > 10; });

instruction: Transform the loop to run in parallel using ParlayLib.
input: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { B[i] = A[i] * A[i]; }
output: auto B = parlay::tabulate(n, [&] (int i) { return A[i] * A[i]; });

instruction: Use ParlayLib to Compute the prefix sum (scan) of squared values in a sequence.
output: auto result = parlay::scan(parlay::map(seq, [] (int x) { return x * x; }));

instruction: Use ParlayLib to Compute the number of even numbers in a sequence.
output: auto evens = parlay::count_if(seq, [] (int x) { return x % 2 == 0; });

instruction: Use ParlayLib to Find all indices in a sequence where the element is greater than its index using parlaylib.
output: auto indices = parlay::filter(parlay::tabulate(seq.size(), [&] (size_t i) { return i; }), [&] (size_t i) { return seq[i] > i; });

instruction: Implement using ParlayLib: Sort a sequence of key-value pairs by key using parlaylib.
output: auto sorted = parlay::sort(pairs, [] (auto a, auto b) { return a.first < b.first; });

instruction: Write a ParlayLib expression to Remove duplicates from a sequence of integers.
output: auto unique_vals = parlay::remove_duplicates(parlay::sort(seq));

instruction: Implement using ParlayLib: Compute the dot product of two sequences.
output: auto dot = parlay::reduce(parlay::tabulate(seq1.size(), [&] (size_t i) {return seq1[i] * seq2[i]; }));

output: auto evens = parlay::count_if(seq, [] (int x) { return x % 2 == 0; });

instruction: Use ParlayLib to Find all indices in a sequence where the element is greater than its index using parlaylib.
output: auto indices = parlay::filter(parlay::tabulate(seq.size(), [&] (size_t i) { return i; }), [&] (size_t i) { return seq[i] > i; });

instruction: Write a ParlayLib expression to Sort a sequence of key-value pairs by key using parlaylib.
output: auto sorted = parlay::sort(pairs, [] (auto a, auto b) { return a.first < b.first; });

instruction: Use ParlayLib to Remove duplicates from a sequence of integers.
output: auto unique_vals = parlay::remove_duplicates(parlay::sort(seq));
